pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing.
pricing.subtitle: Seat-based plans with SIMY token allocation. Scale as your team grows.
pricing.webNote: Create your SIMY account on the web. Subscription checkout is securely handled by Stripe.
pricing.enterprise.desc:
Custom token allocation, dedicated support, SSO, and SLA guarantees.
Built for organizations that need full control.
pricing.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
pricing.faq.a1: Tokens are the currency used by SIMY's AI engine. Each code generation task, review, and pull request consumes tokens based on complexity. Your plan includes a monthly token allocation that resets each billing cycle.
pricing.faq.a2: Yes. Upgrade or downgrade at any time. When upgrading, you'll receive the additional tokens immediately. When downgrading, the change takes effect at the start of your next billing cycle.
pricing.faq.q3: What happens if I run out of tokens?
pricing.faq.a3: If you exhaust your monthly allocation, you can purchase additional token packs or upgrade your plan. Your existing work and data remain fully accessible.
pricing.faq.a4: Monthly token allocations reset each billing cycle and do not roll over. For maximum flexibility, consider the Scale plan or contact us about Enterprise options.
pricing.faq.a5: Yes. Choose annual billing to save 20% compared to monthly pricing. Annual plans are billed upfront for the full year.
pricing.secP: Physical database isolation, end-to-end encryption, and zero-training guarantee on Enterprise plans.
compare.heroP: SIMY stands alone in its own category
compare.cardSubtitle: SIMY stands alone in its own category
compare.ctaP: Try SIMY alongside your current tools and measure the impact.
contact.heroP: Request a demo, ask about Enterprise, or just say hello.
contact.responseNote: We typically respond within 1 business day. For urgent matters, email us directly.
how.heroP: From meeting to merged pull request — four steps, zero prompts.
how.step1P: Connect SIMY to your meeting platform — Zoom, Google Meet, or any conferencing tool. SIMY joins as a silent participant and captures the full conversation.
how.step1L1: Integrates with Zoom, Google Meet, and more
how.step1L3: Encrypted in transit and at rest
how.step2P: SIMY's AI engine transcribes, analyzes, and extracts actionable engineering decisions from the conversation. It identifies tasks, architecture choices, and implementation details.
how.step2L2: Extracts technical decisions and action items
how.step2L3: Maps decisions to your existing codebase
how.step3P: Based on the meeting context and your codebase, SIMY generates production-ready code. Not snippets — complete implementations that follow your team's patterns and conventions.
how.step3L2: Follows your existing code style and patterns
how.step4P: SIMY creates a pull request on GitHub with the generated code, a clear description of what was discussed, and why each change was made. Your team reviews and merges.
how.ctaP: Join the pilot program and turn your next meeting into code.
int.heroP: SIMY connects to the tools your team already uses.
int.githubP: SIMY creates pull requests, runs code reviews, and manages branches directly in your GitHub repositories.
int.slackP: Get real-time notifications when SIMY generates code, creates PRs, or completes reviews. Manage tasks from Slack.
int.zoomP: SIMY joins your Zoom meetings as a bot participant to record, transcribe, and extract engineering tasks automatically.
int.gwsP: Connect Google Meet for recordings and Google Calendar for automatic meeting scheduling.
int.soonP: We're building integrations for more tools your team uses.
sec.heroP: Your code and conversations are protected by design, not afterthought.
sec.transitP: All data is encrypted using TLS 1.3 during transmission between your devices, our servers, and third-party integrations.
sec.restP: Data stored on our servers is encrypted using AES-256. Database backups are also encrypted.
sec.cloudP: Hosted on AWS/GCP with industry-standard security controls, network isolation, and regular security patching.
sec.accessP: Role-based access control (RBAC), multi-factor authentication for internal systems, and principle of least privilege.
sec.compP: We are committed to achieving industry-standard compliance certifications. Our current status:
sec.discP: We take security vulnerabilities seriously. If you discover a security issue, please report it responsibly.
sec.dataNote: Enterprise data is never used for AI training. Standard and Pro plan data may be used to improve our models. See our Privacy Policy for full details.
sec.reportP: Email with details of the vulnerability. We aim to acknowledge reports within 24 hours and provide a resolution timeline within 72 hours.
about.challenge.p1: Engineering teams spend a significant amount of time in meetings — architecture alignment, design reviews, bug triage. Important decisions are made there. But the moment a meeting ends, a different process begins.
about.challenge.p2: Someone interprets the content, organizes it, creates tasks, and converts it into code. This "translation layer" doesn't just slow things down — it degrades the purity of the original decisions.
about.challenge.p3: People don't understand the same conversation the same way. Even when something is decided in a meeting, if someone's interpretation gets inserted along the way, the direction gradually drifts from the original intent.
about.challenge.p4: Furthermore, for humans, the mode of grasping essence through conversation and the mode of writing code, operating systems, and implementing precisely are fundamentally different. The latter carries high cognitive load and often comes with stress. What people should truly focus on is the top 10% of critical decisions that generate 90% of the results.
about.p1: We believe the best code starts with great conversations — and that no one should have to manually translate specs, break down tasks, and convert them into code after those conversations.
about.p2: That's the future SIMY is building.
about.quote: "What's decided in a meeting should ship immediately."
about.solution.p1: SIMY understands your team's natural conversations and transforms them directly into production-ready code, reviews, and pull requests. No prompts needed. No post-meeting rework. The moment a meeting decides what to build, that decision starts moving toward shipping.
about.solution.p2: SIMY removes the translation layer that existed between conversation and implementation. As a result, teams can focus not on "explaining" or "translating," but on "making truly important decisions."
about.solution.p3: By making conversation the interface, SIMY preserves the purity of decisions and connects them directly to delivery. It transforms every meeting from a mere discussion into a starting point for shipping.
about.valA.p1: When a meeting decides what to build, code ships immediately. SIMY's goal is to bring the time between decision and implementation as close to zero as possible.
about.valA.p2: Humans should focus on the top 10% of critical matters that generate 90% of the results. Task translation, bridging to implementation, and operational overhead should be handled by the system wherever possible. Success is measured not by how much you wrote, but by how quickly and correctly you shipped.
about.valB.p1: People may look at the same thing, but they don't understand it the same way. That's why when someone interprets, summarizes, and outputs what was decided in a meeting, intent gradually drifts.
about.valB.p2: SIMY minimizes that drift by generating output directly from conversation. It preserves the background, context, and nuance of decisions as much as possible, shaping what users truly need with high purity.
about.valC.h: C. The Best Code Starts with Great Conversations
about.valC.p1: When humans think deeply and make good decisions, much of it happens in conversation. On the other hand, writing code, operating systems, and controlling things precisely requires a different kind of focus and load.
about.valC.p2: We want to eliminate the very state where people must constantly switch between these two modes. Toward a world where code naturally emerges from great conversations. Humans think, dialogue, and decide. SIMY connects that to reliable shipping.
careers.heroP: We're redefining how software gets built. Come help us make every meeting count.
careers.hiring.p: We're looking for engineers who want to redefine how software gets built. Send us your story.
careers.perk1P: Work from anywhere in the world.
careers.perk2P: Direct impact on the product and company direction.
careers.perk3P: Small team, big impact. No bureaucracy.
careers.why.p1: We're a small, focused team building at the intersection of AI, natural language understanding, and software engineering. Every person on the team has direct impact on the product and the company's direction.
careers.why.p2: We believe the future of coding isn't typing — it's talking. And we're building the tools to make that future real.
pr.aboutP: SIMY is a developer productivity company building AI infrastructure that bridges the gap between team communication and software delivery. The company's platform captures workplace conversations, maintains a structured knowledge base of engineering decisions, and autonomously generates production-ready code. For more information, visit <a href="https://simy.one" style="color:#0057ff;text-decoration:none">simy.one
pr.h2.4: How SIMY Differs from Existing AI Coding Tools
pr.p1: SIMY, a developer productivity company, today announced the launch of its AI-powered software development platform that autonomously generates production-ready code directly from workplace conversations. The system captures discussions across Slack, Microsoft Teams, Gmail, and Zoom, automatically creating and maintaining a structured database of engineering tasks. Generated code is submitted as pull requests to the team's GitHub repository — eliminating the gap between what teams decide in meetings and what gets built.
pr.p10: AI coding assistants such as GitHub Copilot, OpenAI Codex, and Claude Code have significantly accelerated individual developer productivity. However, these tools share a common architectural assumption: the engineer is the interface. A team discusses what to build, an engineer interprets that discussion, and then the engineer translates their interpretation into a prompt. The AI generates code based on that prompt alone. This workflow introduces variability at two critical points — the engineer's comprehension of the team's intent and their ability to express it as a precise instruction.
pr.p11: SIMY removes this translation burden from engineers. Because the platform captures the original conversations directly, the AI receives the raw context — the full thread of discussion, the trade-offs debated, the edge cases flagged — and works from that primary source rather than a second-hand summary. Engineers no longer need to spend time re-encoding what the team already discussed into a prompt; they can focus on the architectural and design decisions where their expertise matters most.
pr.p12: The platform goes further than literal transcription. SIMY's AI engine analyzes the organizational context surrounding each conversation — what problem the team is ultimately trying to solve, who the end users and personas of the system are, and what business objectives are at stake. Based on this analysis, the AI drafts a refined specification for the team to review before writing any code: surfacing ambiguities for resolution, inferring likely requirements from established patterns, and flagging where the implementation may diverge from the product's broader purpose. The team retains full approval authority over what gets built — but the specification they approve is sharper than what any individual could draft alone.
pr.p13: SIMY proposes a new operating model for software teams. In every engineering role, roughly 90% of time has been spent on execution tasks — interpreting requirements, writing boilerplate, filing status updates — while the remaining 10% of decisions drive 90% of product outcomes. SIMY automates the former and augments the latter: the right column of the table below is not work that humans do alone, but work that humans do with AI as a thinking partner, leveraging the platform's organizational context to make sharper, faster, better-informed decisions.
pr.p14: <em>This is not a forecast — it is the operating model SIMY enables today. The shift from execution to judgment defines the AI-native engineering organization.
pr.p15: Data collection is strictly opt-in: teams select exactly which channels, threads, and meeting recordings SIMY may access, and can revoke access or delete indexed data at any time. The platform employs physically isolated database instances for each customer, communication-based access controls that restrict data visibility to original conversation participants, end-to-end encryption, and a zero-training guarantee ensuring that customer code and communication data are never used to train external AI models.
pr.p16: SIMY is currently accepting participants for its pilot program. Pilot partners receive initial credits to evaluate the platform, with usage-based pricing thereafter. The platform supports integration with Slack, Microsoft Teams, Gmail, Zoom, and GitHub.
pr.p2: At the core of SIMY is a proprietary conversation-capture engine. Teams choose which channels to connect — specific Slack channels, Teams groups, Gmail threads, or Zoom meeting recordings — and the engine indexes only those opted-in sources. Unlike conventional AI coding assistants that require engineers to write detailed prompts, SIMY extracts requirements, specifications, and technical decisions directly from the natural flow of team discussions.
pr.p3: The captured data feeds into what the company calls its Action Database — a continuously updated repository of engineering tasks, each enriched with full conversational context: who requested the feature, what constraints were discussed, which architectural decisions were made, and why specific trade-offs were chosen.
pr.p4: Each action also carries a clearly defined Done State — a conversation-derived specification of what "complete" looks like for that particular task. SIMY extracts acceptance conditions directly from team discussions, including expected behavior, edge cases, performance thresholds, and integration points. This enables the AI to verify its own output against the team's actual expectations before submitting a pull request.
pr.p5: Together, the contextual layer and the Done State definition ensure that generated code reflects not just what needs to be built but why and when it is done — preserving institutional knowledge that is typically lost between meetings and implementation, and dramatically reducing review cycles and rework.
pr.p6: In internal benchmarks conducted across 30 production engineering tasks on a single mid-size codebase (Python/TypeScript, ~50k lines) — including database migrations, full-stack feature development, and API integrations — SIMY achieved an <strong>86.7% first-shot success rate
pr.p7: The average cost per autonomous task execution was $1.95, compared to an estimated $50 or more per hour for a software engineer performing the equivalent work manually.
pr.p8: SIMY operates in three stages. First, the platform connects to a team's existing communication infrastructure and automatically captures specifications, requirements, and decisions from ongoing conversations. Second, the AI engine processes captured data, decomposing discussions into discrete, actionable engineering tasks while preserving dependencies and priority assignments. Third, cloud-based AI agents autonomously write code, execute tests, and submit pull requests to the team's GitHub repository — ready for human review and merge.
pr.p9: The platform also includes a Digital Twin feature — an AI agent that can answer questions about project progress, team priorities, and the rationale behind specific engineering decisions, drawing on the full history of captured conversations.
pr.subtitle: Autonomous code generation from workplace conversations shifts every engineering role from execution to decision-making — delivering 17.7x more pull requests per engineer with 86.7% first-shot accuracy

pr.th.execute: Execute with AI as a Thinking Partner
pr.title: SIMY Launches Platform for Building AI-Native Engineering Organizations
press.aboutP: SIMY, developed by AwakApp Inc., is an AI-powered platform that auto-generates production-ready code, reviews, and pull requests from meeting recordings. By treating natural conversation as the programming interface, SIMY eliminates the gap between engineering decisions and code delivery — enabling teams to ship 17.7x more pull requests per engineer with a 96.3% code review pass rate.
press.heroP: Everything you need to write about SIMY.
press.inquiryP: For press inquiries, interviews, or media requests, contact us at <a class="eml" data-u="press" data-d="simy.one" style="color:var(--primary);text-decoration:none">
status.issuesP: Issues? Contact us at <a class="eml" data-u="tech" data-d="simy.one" style="color:var(--primary);text-decoration:none">
cmpx.label: COMPARE
cmpx.heroSub: SIMY doesn't replace your existing tools — it eliminates the gap between decisions and execution that every other category of AI tool still leaves open.
cmpx.intro: SIMY vs. the four categories of AI tools enterprises actually use today. Unlike search, PC agents, coding AI, or team chat — SIMY builds the automations itself, with zero prompting.
cmpx.diffH2:
Other tools assist humans.
SIMY builds and executes for them.
cmpx.card1P: AI Search (Glean) — finds information faster. A human still has to read it and decide what to do.
cmpx.card2P: PC Agents, Coding AI, Claude Cowork — accelerate individual or team tasks. A human still has to prompt, instruct, and guide every step.
cmpx.card3P: SIMY — captures decisions from conversations, builds the missing automations itself, and delivers finished results. Humans just review and approve.
cmpx.ctaH: Try SIMY alongside your current tools.
cmpx.ctaP: Measure the impact. No prompts. No setup friction.
cmpx.c6Simy: Grows itself as you use it
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy describes how AwakApp Inc. ("Company," "we," "our," or "us"), a California corporation, collects, uses, discloses, and protects personal information from users of SIMY (the "Service"). By using the Service, you consent to the data practices described herein.
privacy.p1: The Service is intended solely for users 18 years of age or older. We do not knowingly collect information from individuals under 18. If we discover such data, it will be deleted promptly. Age eligibility is determined by self-certification; users bear sole responsibility for misrepresentation.
privacy.h2_1_li1: Account Data: Name, email, authentication credentials, and payment information (processed by third-party providers).
privacy.h2_1_li2: User Content: Text, code, documents, and files you input or create.
privacy.h2_1_li3: Voice and Audio Data: Voice recordings, audio messages, and the resulting transcriptions. We collect metadata such as duration and timestamps.
privacy.h2_2_li1: Technical Data: IP address, device type, operating system, and approximate geographic location.
privacy.h2_2_li2: Usage Data: Features used, pages viewed, and actions taken within the Service.
privacy.h2_2_li3: Cookies: We use cookies to improve user experience and analyze Service performance.
privacy.p2_3: If you connect third-party services (Slack, GitHub, Zoom, Google Workspace, etc.), we receive data as authorized by those platforms' respective permission settings.
privacy.h3_note: Crucial Responsibility: By using voice recording features, you represent and warrant that you have obtained all necessary consents from all parties involved in the conversation, as required by applicable laws (including "all-party consent" states like California). The Company is not responsible for illegal recordings made by users.
privacy.h4: 4. How We Use Your Information (AI Training Policy)
privacy.p4: Our use of User Content for AI development depends on your subscription plan:
privacy.h4_li1: (a) Standard and Pro Plans (Individual): We may use User Content and voice transcriptions to train, fine-tune, and improve our AI models and machine learning algorithms.
privacy.h4_li2: (b) Enterprise and Business Plans: We DO NOT use your User Content or voice data to train our global AI models. Your data is used exclusively to provide the Service to your organization.
privacy.h4_li3: (c) Google User Data: Notwithstanding any other provision, information received from Google APIs will NEVER be used for AI model training, in strict adherence to the Google API User Data Policy's Limited Use requirements.
privacy.h4_li4: (d) Biometric Data: We do NOT use voice data to create biometric voiceprints or for individual authentication.
privacy.p5: We do not sell your personal information. We share data only in these cases:
privacy.h5_li1: Service Providers: Cloud hosting (AWS/GCP), payment processors (Stripe), and analytics tools.
privacy.h5_li2: Legal Requirements: To comply with lawful requests by public authorities or to protect our legal rights.
privacy.h5_li3: Business Transfers: In the event of a merger, sale, or bankruptcy.
privacy.h6_li1: General Data: We retain personal data as long as your account is active. Upon account deletion, we generally delete data within 90 days.
privacy.h6_li2: Exceptions: We may retain data longer if required for legal compliance, dispute resolution, or as part of anonymized/aggregated datasets. Backups may persist for a limited period per our standard cycle.
privacy.h6_li3: AI Models: For Standard/Pro users, data already incorporated into trained models cannot be retroactively deleted.
privacy.p7: Depending on your location (e.g., EEA, UK, California), you may have the following rights:
privacy.h7_li1: Access, Correct, or Delete your personal information.
privacy.h7_li2: Object to or Restrict certain processing activities.
privacy.h7_li3: Data Portability to receive your data in a structured format.
privacy.h7_li4: Opt-Out of AI Training: Standard/Pro users may request to opt-out of future training by emailing info@simy.one.
privacy.h8_note: IMPORTANT: You acknowledge that the Company has NOT yet obtained SOC2 Type II, HIPAA, or PCI DSS certifications.
privacy.h8_li1: You are strictly prohibited from inputting "Highly Sensitive Personal Data" (e.g., health records, credit card numbers, SSNs).
privacy.h8_li2: While we use industry-standard encryption, we do not guarantee absolute security. You accept all inherent risks of online data transmission.
privacy.p9: Your data may be transferred to and processed in the United States. For users in the EEA/UK, we rely on Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) to ensure an adequate level of data protection.
privacy.p10: In the event of a breach posing a risk to your rights, we will notify you via email or through the Service without undue delay, and within 72 hours where required by law (e.g., GDPR).
privacy.p11:
AwakApp Inc.
Privacy Requests: info@simy.one
General Inquiries: info@simy.one
privacy.h12_note: BY USING SIMY, YOU ACKNOWLEDGE AND AGREE THAT:
privacy.h12_o1: You have read and understood this Privacy Policy.
privacy.h12_o2: Enterprise data is NOT used for training; Standard/Pro data IS used.
privacy.h12_o3: The Service is NOT yet SOC2 or HIPAA compliant.
privacy.h12_o4: You are 100% responsible for obtaining recording consents from others.
privacy.h12_o5: You are 18 years of age or older and not in a sanctioned jurisdiction.
privacy.p_end: IF YOU DO NOT AGREE TO THESE TERMS, DO NOT USE THE SERVICE.
terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: These Terms of Use ("Terms") govern your use of SIMY (the "Service"), operated by AwakApp Inc. ("Company," "we," "our," or "us"), a California corporation. By accessing or using the Service, you agree to be bound by these Terms.
terms.p1: These Terms apply to all users. By using the Service, you confirm you have read, understood, and agreed to these Terms and our Privacy Policy. The Company has no obligation to monitor your compliance; you are solely responsible for your use.
terms.p2: By using the Service, you represent that:
terms.h2_li1: You are at least 18 years of age.
terms.h2_li2: You have the legal capacity to enter into binding contracts.
terms.p2_end: Eligibility is determined by self-certification. The Company bears no liability for misrepresentation of age or identity.
terms.p3: You represent that you are not located in a U.S. sanctioned country (e.g., Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Syria) or on any restricted party list (OFAC SDN, etc.). You are solely responsible for compliance with local and international export control laws.
terms.p4: SIMY is an AI-powered engineering and productivity tool that processes conversations (voice/text) to generate code and manage development tasks. Features may change, be limited, or discontinued at any time without notice.
terms.p5_1: You are solely responsible for all content input and for verifying all AI-generated outputs before use.
terms.h5_2_note: IMPORTANT: The Service is NOT currently certified for highly regulated data. You covenant that you will NOT input:
terms.h5_2_li2: Payment Card Information (PCI) or financial credentials.
terms.h5_2_li3: Government-classified information or ITAR-controlled technical data.
terms.h5_2_li4: Passwords, API keys, or security tokens in a plain-text format.
terms.p5_3: You are solely responsible for obtaining all necessary consents from all parties before recording any conversation. You must comply with all applicable wiretapping and eavesdropping laws (e.g., "all-party consent" jurisdictions).
terms.h6: 6. Intellectual Property and User Content License
terms.p6: The Service and its original content are owned by the Company.
terms.h6_1: 6.1 License Grant and AI Training Policy
terms.p6_1: You retain ownership of your "User Content." However, you grant the Company a license to use it to provide and improve the Service. AI training usage depends on your subscription plan:
terms.h6_1_li1: (a) Standard and Pro Plans (Individual): You agree that the Company may use your User Content to train, fine-tune, and improve our AI models and algorithms.
terms.h6_1_li2: (b) Enterprise and Business Plans: The Company will NOT use your User Content to train or improve our global AI models. Your data remains isolated to your organization's use and internal technical support.
terms.h6_1_li3: (c) Anonymized Data: We may use anonymized, aggregated data from all plans for analytical purposes.
terms.p7: AI-generated code and suggestions ("AI Output") may be inaccurate, biased, or contain errors.
terms.h7_note: Human-in-the-Loop: You MUST review and test all AI Output. The Company is not liable for bugs, security vulnerabilities, or system failures caused by AI-generated code.
terms.p8: Fees are non-refundable. Subscriptions auto-renew unless cancelled via account settings or by emailing tech@simy.one.
terms.p9: We integrate with GitHub, Slack, Zoom, etc. We are not responsible for the privacy practices or availability of these third-party platforms.
terms.p10: We may terminate your access at any time for any reason, including suspected policy violations or security risks. Upon termination, the license grant for AI models (for non-enterprise users) survives.
terms.h11_li1: No SOC2/HIPAA/PCI: You acknowledge that the Company has NOT yet obtained SOC2 Type II, HIPAA, or PCI DSS certifications.
terms.h11_li2: No Physical Isolation: Unless specifically agreed in a separate written Enterprise Agreement, the Service does not provide physical database isolation.
terms.h11_li3: Security Risk: While we use industry-standard encryption, we do not guarantee that the Service is immune to unauthorized access or data breaches.
terms.h12_note: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW:
terms.h12_li1: Our total liability is limited to 50% of the amount you paid us in the 12 months preceding the claim. If you paid $0, our liability is $0.
terms.h12_li2: We are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages, including loss of profits, data, or business opportunities.
terms.p13: You agree to indemnify and hold the Company harmless from any claims arising from:
terms.h13_li3: (c) Your input of prohibited data (PHI, PCI, etc.); or
terms.h13_li4: (d) Your failure to obtain recording consent.
terms.p14: These Terms are governed by California law. Any disputes shall be resolved exclusively in the state or federal courts located in San Francisco County, California. You waive your right to a jury trial and class action.
terms.p15: We may update these Terms at any time. Material changes will be notified 30 days in advance. Continued use constitutes acceptance of the new Terms.
terms.h17_li1: You are 18+ and not a sanctioned party.
terms.h17_li2: Enterprise data is NOT used for AI training; Individual/Pro data IS used.
terms.h17_li3: The Service is NOT SOC2/HIPAA compliant at this time.
terms.h17_li4: You have obtained all necessary recording consents.
terms.h17_li5: You accept the strictly limited liability of the Company.
terms.p_end: IF YOU DO NOT AGREE, DO NOT USE THE SERVICE.
home.h1: Meeting ends.Your Twin startsworking.
home.sub: The moment the meeting ends, SIMY drafts the follow-up, deck, CRM note, team update, and next action. You review what matters, then move to the next customer, team, or decision.
home.hero.note: SIMY prepares the work. You approve before anything is sent or shared.
home.hero.item1.p: Context, tone, and next steps are included.
home.hero.item2.p: Story, risks, and asks are ready.
home.hero.item3.p: Each stakeholder gets the version they need.
home.value.label: What starts next
home.value.h: Before you reopen a tab,the next move is already in motion.
home.value.p: Meeting context becomes a send-ready email, proposal outline, CRM memo, and team update. You do not start from zero. You start from approval.
home.flow.need.v: Cost range and rollout path by Friday
home.flow.concern.v: Customer is worried about operational load
home.flow.next.v: Send proposal, update CRM, brief the team
home.flow.share.p: Only the people who need it.
home.samples.label: What one meeting creates
home.samples.h: When the meeting ends,the work waits for your approval.
home.samples.p: SIMY moves the promises from the meeting into work you can send, save, or share.
home.samples.email.p: Tone, context, concerns, and the next step are already reflected.
home.samples.email.out: Thank you for today. I will send the cost range and rollout path by Friday, with two options for reducing operational load.
home.samples.deck.p: A client-ready storyline starts from the conversation, not a blank slide.
home.samples.deck.out: Outline: customer issue, proposed path, risks, decision required, next meeting agenda.
home.samples.crm.p: The signal your team needs is captured before details fade.
home.samples.crm.out: Temperature: high. Concern: migration from current operation. Next action: rough estimate by Friday.
home.samples.team.p: Sales, CS, and leadership each get the version they need.
home.samples.team.out: Route to sales, CS, and leadership. Owner, deadline, customer concern, and required decision are clear.
home.diff.label: Why founders can trust it
home.diff.h: SIMY moves the draft.You keep the judgment.
home.diff.p: SIMY is not a second inbox that creates more work. It prepares the next action from the meeting context, then waits for your review before anything leaves the company.
home.twin.p: Follow-up emails, proposal outlines, CRM notes, stakeholder updates, and next actions are assembled from the meeting.
home.twin.flow: Meeting ends -> Drafts ready -> You approve
home.agent.p: Price, promise, relationship, and final wording stay with the human. SIMY does not send or share without approval.
home.work.label: What your Twin moves
home.work.h: The work keeps moving.Your day opens up.
home.work.sales.h: Capture the decisions,asks, and promises.
home.work.sales.p: SIMY turns the conversation into working context before the thread goes cold.
home.work.deck.p: Decisions, risks, emotion, stakeholder needs, and hidden blockers are surfaced.
home.work.analysis.p: Follow-ups, decks, research, analysis, updates, CRM, and stakeholder briefs move in the background.
home.work.mail.p: Approve, adjust, call, negotiate, decide, or walk into the next conversation prepared.
home.work.share.p: The next person, message, and moment are visible without rebuilding the context.
home.work.ops.p: Responses, misses, and next actions become a system your team can trust.
home.loop.label: How it works
home.loop.1.p: The conversation, promises, concerns, and next actions become context.
home.loop.2.p: Follow-up, deck, CRM note, and internal update are prepared from the meeting.
home.loop.3.p: Sending, sharing, and judgment stay with you. SIMY does not send without approval.
home.loop.4.p: Drafts, decks, research, analysis, updates, and next actions are packaged.
home.loop.5.p: You come back to a draft, decision point, or next conversation with the context intact.
home.proof.label: Use cases after the meeting
home.proof.h: Sales, strategy, customers, and operations keep moving after every meeting.
home.proof.t1.p: Decisions, asks, blockers, and promises are captured.
home.proof.t2.p: Follow-up, deck, research, analysis, and sharing agents move in sequence.
home.proof.t3.p: The follow-up, decision point, or conversation is waiting with context intact.
home.persona.label: Examples beyond engineering
home.persona.ceo.p: Board notes, investor replies, decision logs, and stakeholder updates.
home.persona.sales.p: Follow-up emails, proposal decks, CRM notes, objections, and next steps.
home.persona.consult.p: Issue trees, research briefs, client-ready slides, risks, and action plans.
home.persona.ops.p: Success plans, renewal paths, support context, owners, and internal handoffs.
home.pricing.biz: Pro is the standard plan for teams that want meeting follow-up, customer updates, and internal handoffs to keep moving. Tokens are the usage meter; the job is simple: fewer dropped balls after meetings.
home.final.h: Meeting ends.Your next move is ready.
home.final.p: Create the Twin that starts working the moment the meeting ends. Let the work come back as drafts, briefs, and next actions instead of another hour in tabs.
home.footer.tagline: Meeting ends.Your Twin starts working.
home.footer.copy: SIMY is the AI Twin that carries the work after meetings.
